The worktree is clean, so there may be nothing to implement. I’m waiting on the package test run now; if it passes, I’ll verify the pre-commit gate and then stop without making unnecessary edits.

    15	// newTestBackend returns a Backend rooted at a fresh temp dir for the explicit
    16	// backend name supplied by the caller. The chaos suite uses it so both JSONL
    17	// and axon run through the same Backend interface surface rather than one path
    18	// silently defaulting to JSONL.
    19	func newTestBackend(t *testing.T, backend string) Backend {
    20		t.Helper()
    21		switch backend {
    22		case BackendJSONL:
    23			return newJSONLStore(t)
    24		case BackendAxon:
    25			return newAxonStore(t)
    26		default:
    27			t.Fatalf("unsupported chaos backend %q", backend)
    28			return nil
    29		}
    30	}
    31	
    32	// chaosBackend names a backend constructor for the parameterized chaos suite.
    33	// Per ddx-743bc194, every backend-agnostic chaos scenario must run against both
    34	// the JSONL default and the axon backend so a regression in either path fails
    35	// the same suite.
    36	type chaosBackend struct {
    37		name    string
    38		backend string
    39	}
    40	
    41	func chaosBackends() []chaosBackend {
    42		return []chaosBackend{
    43			{name: "jsonl", backend: BackendJSONL},
    44			{name: "axon", backend: BackendAxon},
    45		}
    46	}
    47	
    48	// forEachChaosBackend runs fn once per backend as a parallel subtest. The
    49	// subtest receives a fresh Backend, so scenarios stay isolated per backend.
    50	func forEachChaosBackend(t *testing.T, fn func(*testing.T, Backend)) {
    51		t.Helper()
    52		for _, bf := range chaosBackends() {
    53			bf := bf
    54			t.Run(bf.name, func(t *testing.T) {
    55				t.Parallel()
    56				fn(t, newTestBackend(t, bf.backend))
    57			})
    58		}
    59	}

    61	// TestChaos_ConcurrentAppendSafety spawns 10 goroutines each creating 10 beads
    62	// against the same store. Verifies all 100 beads exist, no duplicates, no corruption.
    63	func TestChaos_ConcurrentAppendSafety(t *testing.T) {
    64		forEachChaosBackend(t, func(t *testing.T, s Backend) {
    65			const goroutines = 10
    66			const beadsEach = 10
    67			const total = goroutines * beadsEach
    68	
    69			var wg sync.WaitGroup
    70			errCh := make(chan error, total)
    71	
    72			for g := 0; g < goroutines; g++ {
    73				g := g
    74				wg.Add(1)
    75				go func() {
    76					defer wg.Done()
    77					for i := 0; i < beadsEach; i++ {
    78						b := &Bead{Title: fmt.Sprintf("goroutine-%d-bead-%d", g, i)}
    79						if err := s.Create(b); err != nil {
    80							errCh <- fmt.Errorf("goroutine %d create %d: %w", g, i, err)
    81						}
    82					}
    83				}()
    84			}
    85	
    86			wg.Wait()
    87			close(errCh)
    88	
    89			var errs []error
    90			for err := range errCh {
    91				errs = append(errs, err)
    92			}
    93			require.Empty(t, errs, "concurrent creates must not error: %v", errs)
    94	
    95			beads, err := s.ReadAll()
    96			require.NoError(t, err)
    97			assert.Len(t, beads, total, "all %d beads must be present", total)
    98	
    99			// Check for duplicates
   100			seen := make(map[string]int)
   101			for _, b := range beads {
   102				seen[b.ID]++
   103			}
   104			for id, count := range seen {
   105				assert.Equal(t, 1, count, "bead %s appears %d times (duplicate)", id, count)
   106			}
   107	
   108			// Check no corruption: every bead must have a valid ID, title, and status
   109			for _, b := range beads {
   110				assert.NotEmpty(t, b.ID, "bead ID must not be empty")
   111				assert.NotEmpty(t, b.Title, "bead title must not be empty")
   112				assert.Contains(t, []string{StatusOpen, StatusInProgress, StatusClosed}, b.Status,
   113					"bead %s has invalid status %q", b.ID, b.Status)
   114			}
   115		})
   116	}
   117	
   118	// TestChaos_AtomicStatusTransitions spawns 5 goroutines each cycling a single
   119	// bead through open→in_progress→closed→open. After all iterations the bead
   120	// must have a valid status and the store must be fully parseable.
   121	func TestChaos_AtomicStatusTransitions(t *testing.T) {
   122		forEachChaosBackend(t, func(t *testing.T, s Backend) {
   123			b := &Bead{Title: "status-churn"}
   124			require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
   125			id := b.ID
   126	
   127			const goroutines = 5
   128			const iterations = 20
   129	
   130			statuses := []string{StatusOpen, StatusInProgress, StatusClosed}
   131			var wg sync.WaitGroup
   132	
   133			for g := 0; g < goroutines; g++ {
   134				g := g
   135				wg.Add(1)
   136				go func() {
   137					defer wg.Done()
   138					for i := 0; i < iterations; i++ {
   139						next := statuses[(g+i)%len(statuses)]
   140						// Ignore errors: contention may cause transient failures that are
   141						// acceptable (e.g. attempting to set a status that is already set).
   142						// The critical invariant is no corruption.
   143						_ = s.Update(id, func(bead *Bead) {
   144							bead.Status = next
   145						})
   146					}
   147				}()
   148			}
   149	
   150			wg.Wait()
   151	
   152			// Bead must still exist with a valid status
   153			got, err := s.Get(id)
   154			require.NoError(t, err, "bead must be readable after concurrent status updates")
   155			assert.Contains(t, []string{StatusOpen, StatusInProgress, StatusClosed}, got.Status,
   156				"bead must have a valid status after concurrent updates")
   157	
   158			// Store must be parseable end-to-end
   159			allBeads, err := s.ReadAll()
   160			require.NoError(t, err, "store must be parseable after concurrent status transitions")
   161			assert.NotEmpty(t, allBeads, "store must not be empty after updates")
   162	
   163			// Verify no partial writes: every entry must decode cleanly
   164			for _, bead := range allBeads {
   165				assert.NotEmpty(t, bead.ID)
   166				assert.NotEmpty(t, bead.Title)
   167			}
   168		})
   169	}
   170	
   171	// TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseAndAppend reproduces the reported bug:
   172	// one goroutine closes a bead while another creates a new bead concurrently.
   173	// Both operations must persist; neither must clobber the other.
   174	func TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseAndAppend(t *testing.T) {
   175		forEachChaosBackend(t, func(t *testing.T, s Backend) {
   176			// Pre-create the bead that will be closed
   177			existing := &Bead{Title: "to-be-closed"}
   178			require.NoError(t, s.Create(existing))
   179			closeID := existing.ID
   180	
   181			const rounds = 50
   182	
   183			for round := 0; round < rounds; round++ {
   184				// Re-open the bead so we can close it again
   185				require.NoError(t, s.Update(closeID, func(b *Bead) {
   186					b.Status = StatusOpen
   187				}))
   188	
   189				newTitle := fmt.Sprintf("new-bead-round-%d", round)
   190				var newID string
   191	
   192				var wg sync.WaitGroup
   193				wg.Add(2)
   194	
   195				// Goroutine A: close the existing bead
   196				go func() {
   197					defer wg.Done()
   198					_ = s.Close(closeID)
   199				}()
   200	
   201				// Goroutine B: create a new bead
   202				go func() {
   203					defer wg.Done()
   204					nb := &Bead{Title: newTitle}
   205					if err := s.Create(nb); err == nil {
   206						newID = nb.ID
   207					}
   208				}()
   209	
   210				wg.Wait()
   211	
   212				// The new bead must exist
   213				if newID != "" {
   214					got, err := s.Get(newID)
   215					require.NoError(t, err, "round %d: new bead %s must exist", round, newID)
   216					assert.Equal(t, newTitle, got.Title, "round %d: new bead title must match", round)
   217				}
   218	
   219				// The closed bead's status must reflect the close (it may have been
   220				// re-opened by a subsequent round, but within this round, if the close
   221				// completed first and create ran after, the close must be visible).
   222				// The critical check: the closed bead must still exist and be parseable.
   223				closedBead, err := s.Get(closeID)
   224				require.NoError(t, err, "round %d: closed bead must still exist", round)
   225				assert.NotEmpty(t, closedBead.ID, "round %d: closed bead ID must not be empty", round)
   226			}
   227		})
   228	}
   229	
   230	// TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseNotLost is the strict regression test for the bug:
   231	// close must not be lost when a concurrent create happens after the close acquires
   232	// the lock but before it writes. With the directory-lock implementation, the lock
   233	// serializes these operations, so the closed status must always be visible after
   234	// both goroutines complete (if the close finishes last, the status is closed;
   235	// if create finishes last, the store was read AFTER the close, so closed is preserved).
   236	func TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseNotLost(t *testing.T) {

   255				createErr := make(chan error, 1)
   256				createdID := make(chan string, 1)
   257	
   258				go func() {

1	package bead
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"fmt"
     5		"testing"
     6	
     7		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
     8		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
     9	)
    10	
    11	type backendConformanceCase struct {
    12		name    string
    13		backend string
    14	}
    15	
    16	func backendConformanceCases() []backendConformanceCase {
    17		return []backendConformanceCase{
    18			{name: "jsonl", backend: BackendJSONL},
    19			{name: "axon", backend: BackendAxon},
    20		}
    21	}
    22	
    23	func forEachBackendConformanceCase(t *testing.T, fn func(*testing.T, backendConformanceCase)) {
    24		t.Helper()
    25		for _, tc := range backendConformanceCases() {
    26			tc := tc
    27			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
    28				t.Parallel()
    29				fn(t, tc)
    30			})
    31		}
    32	}
    33	
    34	func runBackendConformanceSuite(t *testing.T, tc backendConformanceCase) {
    35		t.Helper()
    36		makeStore := func(t *testing.T) *Store {
    37			t.Helper()
    38			switch tc.backend {
    39			case BackendJSONL:
    40				return newJSONLStore(t)
    41			case BackendAxon:
    42				return newAxonStore(t)
    43			default:
    44				t.Fatalf("unsupported backend %q", tc.backend)
    45				return nil
    46			}
    47		}
    48	
    49		t.Run("create-get", func(t *testing.T) {
    50			t.Parallel()
    51			s := makeStore(t)
    52	
    53			b := &Bead{Title: "first", Description: "round-trip"}
    54			require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
    55			require.NotEmpty(t, b.ID)
    56	
    57			got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
    58			require.NoError(t, err)
    59			assert.Equal(t, "first", got.Title)
    60			assert.Equal(t, "round-trip", got.Description)
    61			assert.Equal(t, StatusOpen, got.Status)
    62		})
    63	
    64		t.Run("update", func(t *testing.T) {
    65			t.Parallel()
    66			s := makeStore(t)
    67	
    68			b := &Bead{Title: "to-update"}
    69			require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
    70	
    71			require.NoError(t, s.Update(b.ID, func(bb *Bead) {
    72				bb.Notes = "added by update"
    73				bb.Priority = 3
    74			}))
    75	
    76			got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
    77			require.NoError(t, err)
    78			assert.Equal(t, "added by update", got.Notes)
    79			assert.Equal(t, 3, got.Priority)
    80		})
    81	
    82		t.Run("claim", func(t *testing.T) {
    83			t.Parallel()
    84			s := makeStore(t)
    85	
    86			b := &Bead{Title: "claimable"}
    87			require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
    88	
    89			require.NoError(t, s.Claim(b.ID, "alice"))
    90			got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
    91			require.NoError(t, err)
    92			assert.Equal(t, StatusInProgress, got.Status)
    93			assert.Equal(t, "alice", got.Owner)
    94			require.NotNil(t, got.Extra)
    95			assert.NotEmpty(t, got.Extra["claimed-at"])
    96	
    97			err = s.Claim(b.ID, "bob")
    98			require.Error(t, err)
    99	
   100			require.NoError(t, s.Unclaim(b.ID))
   101			got, err = s.Get(b.ID)
   102			require.NoError(t, err)
   103			assert.Equal(t, StatusOpen, got.Status)
   104			assert.Empty(t, got.Owner)
   105		})
   106	
   107		t.Run("deps", func(t *testing.T) {
   108			t.Parallel()
   109			s := makeStore(t)
   110	
   111			root := &Bead{Title: "root"}
   112			child := &Bead{Title: "child"}
   113			require.NoError(t, s.Create(root))
   114			require.NoError(t, s.Create(child))
   115	
   116			require.NoError(t, s.DepAdd(child.ID, root.ID))
   117			got, err := s.Get(child.ID)
   118			require.NoError(t, err)
   119			require.Len(t, got.Dependencies, 1)
   120			assert.Equal(t, root.ID, got.Dependencies[0].DependsOnID)
   121	
   122			tree, err := s.DepTree(root.ID)
   123			require.NoError(t, err)
   124			assert.Contains(t, tree, root.ID)
   125	
   126			require.NoError(t, s.DepRemove(child.ID, root.ID))
   127			got, err = s.Get(child.ID)
   128			require.NoError(t, err)
   129			assert.Empty(t, got.Dependencies)
   130		})
   131	
   132		t.Run("events-close", func(t *testing.T) {
   133			t.Parallel()
   134			s := makeStore(t)
   135	
   136			b := &Bead{Title: "with-events"}
   137			require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
   138	
   139			for i := 0; i < 3; i++ {
   140				require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{
   141					Kind:    "test",
   142					Summary: fmt.Sprintf("event-%d", i),
   143					Body:    fmt.Sprintf("body-%d", i),
   144					Actor:   "tester",
   145				}))
   146			}
   147	
   148			events, err := s.Events(b.ID)
   149			require.NoError(t, err)
   150			require.Len(t, events, 3)
   151			for i, e := range events {
   152				assert.Equal(t, fmt.Sprintf("event-%d", i), e.Summary)
   153			}
   154	
   155			require.NoError(t, s.Close(b.ID))
   156			got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
   157			require.NoError(t, err)
   158			assert.Equal(t, StatusClosed, got.Status)
   159			events, err = s.Events(b.ID)
   160			require.NoError(t, err)
   161			assert.Len(t, events, 3)
   162		})
   163	}
   164	
   165	func TestBackendConformance(t *testing.T) {
   166		forEachBackendConformanceCase(t, func(t *testing.T, tc backendConformanceCase) {
   167			runBackendConformanceSuite(t, tc)
   168		})
   169	}
